questions.question: What does the architect role own in this factory shape?
options: Reviewing the merged diff for hallucinations
Reading the issue and emitting a plan with subtasks that have explicit interfaces
Running the test suite in a clean sandbox
Force-merging branches when coders disagree
questions.explanation: Role specialization assigns specific capabilities to agents: Planner (breaks down tasks), Critic (reviews code), Executor (implements changes), and Verifier (tests and validates results).

questions.question: Why does each coder work in its own git worktree plus a Daytona sandbox?
options: Worktrees are faster to clone than branches
Daytona is the only sandbox that supports SSH
Isolated working trees let N coders implement subtasks in parallel without stepping on shared files
The architect requires worktree IDs
questions.explanation: Task decomposition uses hierarchical planning to split high-level requirements into atomic subtasks with clear dependencies and success criteria for each agent.

questions.question: What constraint is placed on the reviewer to keep it honest?
options: It can only run after the tester signs off
It cannot approve diffs it authored or proposed
It must approve every diff it reads
It must be the same model as the architect
questions.explanation: Communication protocols define how agents share information: shared state objects for plan status, message passing for handoffs, and consensus mechanisms for decisions.

questions.question: Why does the rubric weigh token efficiency against a single-agent baseline?
options: Multi-agent is always cheaper
Role boundaries add summary and handoff context, so the real question is whether the factory wins per dollar
Single-agent has no observability story
Token cost is unrelated to performance
questions.explanation: Coordination patterns include sequential workflows (agents work in series), parallel swarms (agents work on independent subtasks), and debate topologies (agents propose and critique solutions).

questions.question: Which artifact is produced from each failed issue during the post-mortem?
options: A new architect plan template
An updated A2A protocol spec
A force-push log
A handoff-failure histogram identifying which role boundary broke (plan, merge, review, test)
questions.explanation: The capstone measures task completion rate (percentage of requirements fully implemented), coordination overhead (time spent on agent communication vs actual work), and role effectiveness (contribution of each agent type).

questions.question: Which evaluation set does the capstone use to score pass@1 across roles?
options: SWE-bench Pro 50-issue subset
HumanEval-Java
MT-Bench-v2
MigrationBench
questions.explanation: Failure mode analysis identifies bottlenecks (slow agent responses), deadlocks (circular dependencies), and miscommunication (ambiguous handoffs) with remediation strategies.
